steps.title: Mark the correction
steps.content: The first sentence says kyudo was not a medal sport. The next sentence clarifies that kyudo still appeared at the Games, but in a different role: a demonstration sport. The transition must correct or qualify the first impression.

steps.title: Reject wrong relationships
steps.content: Choice A signals a consequence, but the second sentence is not a result. Choice C signals simple addition. Choice D signals an example, but the second sentence is a qualification. The remaining transition means "instead" or "on the contrary." The answer is B.
